qa_pairs.question: What should a user do if they encounter an "Error: No valid host was found" message when trying to launch a virtual machine (VM) with a specific availability zone?
qa_pairs.answer: If you receive this "not enough hosts" error and require a particular availability zone, you should send an email to cloud@tech.alliancecan.ca. Be sure to include the cloud name, project name, the troubleshooting steps you have taken thus far, and the reason why you need that specific availability zone.

qa_pairs.question: What are the initial troubleshooting steps if a user cannot connect to their virtual machine (VM) or a service running on it?
qa_pairs.answer: First, check the System Status page to see if there is an incident affecting your hosting cloud. If an incident is reported, wait for it to be resolved before trying to connect again. If there's no incident, try logging in to the OpenStack dashboard for your cloud project. If you cannot reach the login page for your cloud, verify your internet connectivity; if you have connectivity but still can't reach the login page, submit a ticket to cloud@tech.alliancecan.ca.

qa_pairs.question: How can a user verify if their virtual machine (VM) is running from the cloud dashboard and what actions can be taken if it isn't?
qa_pairs.answer: After logging in to your cloud dashboard, navigate to the "Instances" screen from the left-side menu. Look at the "Power State" for your VM; it should be "Running". If it's not (e.g., "Shut Down"), try to restart it using the "Actions" menu on the right-hand side by selecting "Start Instance" or "Resume Instance". You should also check the "Action Log" and "log" tabs from the instance's details screen for any error messages that might explain why it was taken out of the running state.
